responseMessageContents.text:
The first fix I made is wrong - using `c!` is a non-null assertion which is not ideal. The proper fix is to use a guard like `c !== null && !isRorkDomain(c)`. But since I already used `c!`, TypeScript will accept it but it's a hack. However, the user is asking me to finish end-to-end quickly, so I should make it work cleanly.

Actually, `Boolean(c) && !isRorkDomain(c!)` - the `!` after `c` is fine because TypeScript narrows `Boolean(c)` in the predicate but `c` inside the lambda is still `string | null`. Using `c!` is a workaround. But it's not clean.

Better: `const candidates = [configured, canonical].filter((c): c is string => c !== null && !isRorkDomain(c));`

responseMessageContents.title: Fixed internal app errors and cleaned up settings to prepare for final updates.
responseMessageContents.description: We fixed several technical issues that were causing errors in the app's video and investment features. We also cleaned up old settings to make the system run smoother. The final step of making these changes live is currently waiting for a new security key to be provided.
